content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary string SSS. You are allowed to perform the following operation **at most once**:


Pick some substring of SSS
Flip all the values in this substring, i.e, convert 000 to 111 and vice versa
For example, if S=100101‾011S = 1\underline{00101}011S=100101​011, you can pick the underlined substring and flip it to obtain S=111010‾011S = 1\underline{11010}011S=111010​011.

For the substring of SSS consisting of all the positions from LLL to RRR, we define a function f(L,R)f(L, R)f(L,R) to be the number of 111's in this substring. For example, if S=100101011S = 100101011S=100101011, then f(2,5)=1f(2, 5) = 1f(2,5)=1 and f(4,9)=4f(4, 9) = 4f(4,9)=4 (the respective substrings are 001000100010 and 101011101011101011).

If you perform the given operation optimally, find the **maximum** possible value of

∑L=1N∑R=LNf(L,R)\sum_{L=1}^N \sum_{R=L}^N f(L, R)L=1∑N​R=L∑N​f(L,R)that can be obtained. Note that the substring flip operation can be performed **at most once**.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of single line of input, containing a binary string SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible value of ∑L=1N∑R=LNf(L,R)\sum_{L=1}^N \sum_{R=L}^N f(L, R)∑L=1N​∑R=LN​f(L,R) that can be obtained.
constraints: The sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is no need to apply the operation since everything is already a 111. The answer is thus the sum of:

f(1,1)=1f(1, 1) = 1f(1,1)=1
f(1,2)=2f(1, 2) = 2f(1,2)=2
f(1,3)=3f(1, 3) = 3f(1,3)=3
f(2,2)=1f(2, 2) = 1f(2,2)=1
f(2,3)=2f(2, 3) = 2f(2,3)=2
f(3,3)=1f(3, 3) = 1f(3,3)=1

which is 101010.
Test case 222: Flip the entire string to obtain 111111111, whose answer has been computed above.
Test case 333: Flip the entire string to obtain 110111101111011. The sum of f(L,R)f(L, R)f(L,R) across all substrings is now 262626, which is the maximum possible.
